methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
DustPhotonics is an acquired-stage company in Chips & Semiconductors founded by Ronnie Cohen and Arik Sandler, having raised $75M+ to date. The valuation landscape includes 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers spanning the sector, with notable private companies including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Traditional triangulation methods face material constraints. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable: while 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, none satisfy narrow filters requiring same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier datapoints in sufficient volume (minimum 3 required). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales is similarly blocked—the public semiconductor universe trades at a 24.2x median multiple across the three tickers, but DustPhotonics' revenue is not on file, precluding meaningful application of this metric. Private companies rarely disclose revenue, limiting the utility of revenue-based multiples.

The absence of sufficient narrow peer data and missing target revenue means standard quantitative valuation methods do not yield a defensible range for this company. Any valuation estimate would require either disclosed revenue figures or a materially broader peer set accepting greater heterogeneity in stage and business model. The $75M+ raised provides a floor on capital invested but does not constitute a valuation estimate. A credible range would require either additional target-specific data or acceptance of wider comparables bands with explicit uncertainty flagging.
